concepts.title: Retirement vs Death of a Partner
concepts.description: On retirement of a partner, the remaining partners are required to purchase the goodwill of the retired partner by capitalising it in the books of the firm. However, on the death of a partner, the amount of goodwill is not capitalised.

concepts.title: Revaluation of Assets and Liabilities
concepts.description: When a partner retires or dies, the assets and liabilities of the firm are revalued and the amount of revaluation is transferred to the capital account of the remaining partners in the ratio of their new profit-sharing ratio.

concepts.title: Method of Valuation of Assets and Liabilities
concepts.description: The method of valuation of assets and liabilities is the average of the values at which they were taken over by the remaining partners and the values at which they were sold to them.

concepts.title: Adjustment of Goodwill
concepts.description: When a partner retires or dies, the adjustment of goodwill is done by debiting or crediting the goodwill account of the retiring or deceased partner according to the new profit-sharing ratio.

concepts.title: Incorrect Treatment of Goodwill
concepts.description: Students often incorrectly treat goodwill as an asset and not as a reserve. Goodwill is a reserve and should be treated as such in the books of the firm.

concepts.title: Steps Involved in Reconstitution of Partnership
concepts.description: The steps involved in reconstitution of partnership are: (i) Calculation of new profit-sharing ratio, (ii) Revaluation of assets and liabilities, (iii) Adjustment of goodwill, (iv) Accounting treatment for the retiring or deceased partner.

concepts.title: Mnemonic for Accounting Treatment of Goodwill
concepts.description: The mnemonic 'DEBIT' can be used to remember the accounting treatment of goodwill: D - Debit goodwill account (when partner retires), E - Equate goodwill account with partner's capital (when partner dies), B - Balance goodwill account with partner's capital (when partner retires), I - Increase goodwill account (when partner dies), T - Transfer goodwill account to partner's capital (when partner retires)

concepts.title: Adjustment of Accumulated Profits and Losses
concepts.description: The accumulated profits and losses are adjusted in the ratio of the new profit-sharing ratio when a partner retires or dies.

concepts.title: Accounting Treatment of Assets and Liabilities
concepts.description: The assets and liabilities of the firm are valued at the market value or book value, whichever is less, when a partner retires or dies.
